activation_results.reason: Input matches the description verbatim — canonical use case.
Synonym swap (Evaluate vs Assess) preserves the core SRE-maturity intent.
SSH key forwarding is unrelated to SRE maturity assessment.
Generic meta-question about skills, not an SRE audit request.

criterion_results.text: No American spellings present in the narrative (color, optimize, behavior, organize, center, defense, license as a verb, analyze) — Australian or neutral forms both pass
criterion_results.evidence: None of the flagged American spellings appear in the narrative.

criterion_results.text: Assessment is bounded — explicit scope, explicit out-of-scope
criterion_results.evidence: Title names target 'acme-api' and mode 'live' but there is no explicit scope/out-of-scope section; 'Suppressed: None' is not a scope declaration.

criterion_results.text: Report includes the canonical sections: 'TL;DR', 'Dimension Heatmap'
criterion_results.evidence: '## TL;DR' and '## Dimension Heatmap' headings both present near the top of the artefact.

criterion_results.text: Artefact references the input path or target by name (no placeholder paths)
criterion_results.evidence: Title references 'acme-api'; body references concrete paths runbooks/, docs/postmortems/, slo.yaml.

judge_result.notes: Artefact satisfies the canonical sectioning, target naming, and spelling conventions, but lacks an explicit scope/out-of-scope statement bounding the assessment. Mode and target are stated in the header, however nothing enumerates what services or dimensions were deliberately excluded. Recommend adding a 'Scope' block listing in-scope services and explicit exclusions to fully meet the bounded-assessment criterion.
